popularStory.headline:
History of Gundred Countess of Surrey
(about 1050 – 1085
popularStory.description: Gundred or Gundreda (Latin: Gundrada) (died 27 May 1085) was the Flemish-born wife of an early Norman baron, William de Warenne, 1st Earl of Surrey. She and her husband established Lewes Priory in Sus …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Gundred de St Omer於1051年出生在法國福藍德 福藍薩時，她的父親Gerbod II de Saint-Omer de Saint-Bertin52歲，母親Ada Oosterzele Earl of Chester52歲。她於1077年與William de Warenne 1st Earl of Surrey在法國諾門迪結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1085年5月27日在英國諾福爾可卡素 阿可享年34歲，埋葬在英國素可列維斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gundred de St Omer于1051年出生在法国福藍德 福藍薩时，她的父亲Gerbod II de Saint-Omer de Saint-Bertin52岁，母亲Ada Oosterzele Earl of Chester52岁。她于1077年与William de Warenne 1st Earl of Surrey在法国諾門迪结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1085年5月27日在英国諾福爾可卡素 阿可享年34岁，埋葬在英国素可列維斯。

variants.name: Dey

English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the numerous places in France so called from the dedication of their churches to St. Jean (  see  John  ).
2  Americanized form of French   St  .   Jean  .

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Jean
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Francois, Marcel, Yves, Andre, Jacques, Georges, Michel, Philippe, Serge, Yvon, Cecile.

French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), Mauritian, and English (of French Huguenot origin): from the male personal name Jean, French form of John . This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Joseph and Pierre ). It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in the Dominican Republic, Saint Lucia, Saint Martin, and Bahamas).
English: habitational name for someone from Genoa in Italy; compare   Janeway  .

nameMeanings.name: Omer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ahmed, Mohamed, Mohammed, Tarig, Mohammad, Murad, Abdirahman, Abdul, Abdulla, Abdulrahim, Ali, Amin.
Muslim: variant of   Omar  .
German: variant of   Ohmer  .

variants.name: Omar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mohammed, Mohamed, Ali, Omar, Ahmed, Ahmad, Hassan, Abdul, Husam, Khalid, Mohammad, Mohd.

Muslim: from the Arabic personal name Omar of uncertain etymology, found almost exclusively among Sunni Muslims. It is thought to be related to Arabic ʿāmir ‘prosperous, full of life, large, substantial’ (see Amir 2). The root word ʿumr means ‘life’. ʿUmar bin al-Khaṭṭāb was the second of the ‘rightly guided’ caliphs (ruled 634–44); he was known by the title al-Fārūq ‘the distinguisher’, i.e. one who distinguishes between truth and falsehood. Compare Omer and Umar .
Catalan: topographic name for someone who lived by a group of elm trees, from the collective form of om ‘elm’.

nameMeanings.name: Gundred
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Gundry  .

variants.name: Gundry
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from Gondri, Gundric, an Old French personal name introduced to Britain by the Normans, composed of the ancient Germanic elements gund ‘battle’ + rīc ‘power(ful)’.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

en.lifeSummary: When Gundred de St Omer was born in 1051, in French Flanders, France, her father, Gerbod II de Saint-Omer de Saint-Bertin, was 52 and her mother, Ada Oosterzele Earl of Chester, was 52. She married William de Warenne 1st Earl of Surrey in 1077, in Normandy, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 27 May 1085, in Castle Acre, Norfolk, England, at the age of 34, and was buried in Lewes, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gundred de St Omer est née en 1051, à Flandre française, France, son père, Gerbod II de Saint-Omer de Saint-Bertin, avait 52 ans et sa mère, Ada Oosterzele Earl of Chester, avait 52 ans. Elle a épousé William de Warenne 1st Earl of Surrey en 1077, à Normandie, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 27 mai 1085, à Castle Acre, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 34 ans, et a été enterrée à Lewes, Sussex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gundred de St Omer 1051 in Flandre française, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gerbod II de Saint-Omer de Saint-Bertin 52 und ihre Mutter Ada Oosterzele Earl of Chester 52. Sie hat 1077 in Normandie, Frankreich William de Warenne 1st Earl of Surrey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 27. Mai 1085 im Alter von 34 Jahren in Castle Acre, Norfolk, England und wurde in Lewes, Sussex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gundred de St Omer è nata il nel 1051 nel Flandre française, Francia, sua padre, Gerbod II de Saint-Omer de Saint-Bertin, aveva 52 anni e sua madre, Ada Oosterzele Earl of Chester, aveva 52 anni. Lei sposato William de Warenne 1st Earl of Surrey nel 1077, nel Normandie, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 27 maggio 1085, nel Castle Acre, Norfolk, England, all'età di 34 anni, e fu sepolta a Lewes, Sussex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Gundred de St Omerが1051年にフランスフランダー フランcアイゼで生まれた時，父Gerbod II de Saint-Omer de Saint-Bertinは52歳，母Ada Oosterzele Earl of Chesterは52歳であった。1077年にフランスノーマンディでWilliam de Warenne 1st Earl of Surreyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1085年5月27日にイギリスノーフォルクカストル アクアで34歳で死去し，イギリススセックスルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Gundred de St Omer(이)가 1051년 프랑스 플랜드르 프랜c아이스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gerbod II de Saint-Omer de Saint-Bertin(은)는 52세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ada Oosterzele Earl of Chester(은)는 52세였다. 그녀는 1077년 프랑스 노먼디에서 William de Warenne 1st Earl of Surrey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1085년 5월 27일 영국 노르폴크 카스틀 악르에서 34세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 루스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gundred de St Omer nasceu em 1051, em Flandre française, França, seu pai, Gerbod II de Saint-Omer de Saint-Bertin, tinha 52 anos e sua mãe, Ada Oosterzele Earl of Chester, tinha 52 anos. Ela casou-se com William de Warenne 1st Earl of Surrey em 1077, em Normandie, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 27 de maio de 1085, em Castle Acre, Norfolk, Inglaterra, com 34 anos, e foi sepultada em Lewes, Sussex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gundred de St Omer родилась в 1051 г. в местности фландр франcеис, Франция, её отцу по имени Gerbod II de Saint-Omer de Saint-Bertin было 52 года, а матери, которую звали Ada Oosterzele Earl of Chester, 52. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William de Warenne 1st Earl of Surrey в 1077 г. в местности норманди, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 27 мая 1085 г. в местности кастл акр, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 34 лет и была похоронена в местности львесь, суссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gundred de St Omer nació en 1051, en Flandre française, Francia, su papá, Gerbod II de Saint-Omer de Saint-Bertin, tenía 52 años y su mamá, Ada Oosterzele Earl of Chester, tenía 52 años. Se casó con William de Warenne 1st Earl of Surrey en 1077, en Normandie, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 27 de mayo de 1085, en Castle Acre, Norfolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 34 años, y fue sepultada en Lewes, Sussex, Inglaterra.
